Title: CPO prices to range between RM4,400 and RM4,650 in July on El Nino risks
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Crude palm oil prices are expected to range between RM4,400 and RM4,650 per tonne in July, supported by a tighter supply outlook in Indonesia and growing concerns over El Niño risks, the Malaysian Palm Oil Council (MPOC) said.
MPOC said that while oil palm plantations in Malaysia and Indonesia have not been impacted as of June, the possibility of a stronger El Niño from July or August is increasing.
"A strong El Niño could reduce rainfall and bring drier conditions across Southeast Asia, Australia and India, although the impact on palm oil yields and production would only become evident after a lag of nine to 12 months," it said in a statement.
It added that upside potential for prices could be limited by high vegetable oil stockpiles in major importing markets.
The council noted that biodiesel margins have become less favourable, as gasoil prices in the futures market have dropped below palm oil prices.
Malaysia's palm oil output fell by 6.9 per cent month-on-month to 1.51 million tonnes in May, as oil palm trees entered a temporary resting period after recording above-average production levels between October 2025 and March 2026.
The decline was also attributed to fewer harvesting days, as May saw two public holidays compared with none in April.
MPOC said the weaker export performance in May was largely expected, as subdued buying activity in March and April amid heightened price volatility was likely to result in lower shipments during May and June.
"Despite the monthly decline, cumulative exports from January to May 2026 increased by 783,000 tonnes or 13.8 per cent.
"The largest increases were recorded in exports to India, Kenya and Vietnam, which collectively rose by 749,000 tonnes," it said.
MPOC said the Sub-Saharan Africa and Asean markets are continuing to gain importance as key growth destinations for Malaysian palm oil.
During the first five months of 2026, the two regions accounted for 36 per cent of Malaysia's total palm oil exports, up from 25 per cent recorded five years earlier in 2022.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: JPA offers 640 sponsorship slots for outstanding SPM leavers
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Public Services Department (PSD) is offering 640 sponsorships to outstanding Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) leavers under its continuous effort to strengthen national talent development.
The initiative, aligned with the Madani government aspirations, aims to ensure top students continue to receive access to quality education at leading local and international higher learning institutions.
The department said this was part of its ongoing efforts to develop national talent in strategic and critical fields to support the national development agenda.
For this year, 30 places are offered under the National Sponsorship Programme and 200 slots under the SPM Leavers Special Programme.
A total of 140 slots are allocated for the Special Programme for Japan, Korea, France and Germany (JKPJ), while the JPA-Mara Special Programme (PKJM) involves 270 slots.
"The sponsorships cover various strategic and critical fields, including engineering, science and technology, as well as social sciences.
"Through these programmes, students will have the opportunity to pursue their studies at prominent educational institutions locally and abroad, including the United States, Australia, Japan, South Korea, France, Germany, the United Kingdom and New Zealand," PSD said in a statement today.
Applicants can check their results online from 10am today until 5pm on June 22 through the department's system.
Successful students will receive funding under the Academic Merit-Based Convertible Loan introduced by PSD in June last year.
"The implementation of the loan reflects the PSD's commitment to recognising academic excellence and encouraging sponsorship recipients to continue achieving their best throughout their studies.
"PSD congratulates all students who successfully received the 2026 sponsorship offers. This success is a recognition of the hard work and excellence they have demonstrated," it said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia's competitiveness ranking surge signals stronger economic fundamentals: Economists
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's improved standing in the International Institute for Management Development (IMD) 2026 World Competitiveness Ranking reflects the country's strengthening economic fundamentals.
This has been supported by robust trade competitiveness, gains in government and business efficiency, as well as growing opportunities in the technology sector, economists said.
SPI Asset Management managing partner Stephen Innes said Malaysia's rise to 15th place among 70 economies in 2026, from 23rd previously, was an encouraging signal, as it reflected more than just a short-term growth rebound.
"The artificial intelligence (AI) and semiconductor boom is clearly an important tailwind. Malaysia is well-positioned across the electrical and electronics supply chain, as well as in advanced packaging and data-centre investment, which makes it a natural beneficiary of global supply-chain diversification," he told Bernama.
Innes said the tourism sector was also supporting the country's competitiveness through stronger visitor arrivals and higher services receipts, while political stability and a more predictable policy environment should further strengthen investor confidence. "The broader message is that Malaysia is becoming a more credible regional platform for technology investment, manufacturing and services," he added.
He noted that a key challenge would be translating the current momentum into sustained productivity gains, stronger innovation, improved skills development and higher-value domestic investment, rather than relying solely on the favourable global semiconductor cycle.
Juwai IQI global chief economist Shan Saeed described Malaysia's improved ranking as one of the most remarkable competitiveness gains among major economies globally.  He noted that the country's ability to combine rising competitiveness with macroeconomic stability remains one of its most underappreciated strengths within Asean, despite an increasingly fragmented global environment.
He said Malaysia's upward trajectory reflected an economy steadily enhancing its productive capacity, strengthening investor confidence and increasing its strategic relevance within the global economy.
"The foundations underpinning this progress are substantial.  Malaysia's economy now exceeds RM2 trillion, while its capital market has reached a record RM4.3 trillion, equivalent to 210 per cent of gross domestic product (GDP), making it one of the deepest and most sophisticated capital markets in the emerging world," said Shan.
Malaysia's leadership in Islamic finance, resilient banking system, world-class infrastructure and strong integration into global value chains spanning semiconductors, electronics, advanced manufacturing and high-value services also represented enduring structural strengths that continue to support long-term investment and economic resilience, he added.
Besides that, Malaysia's strategic location within Asean enhances its attractiveness as multinational companies diversify their supply chains and increase investments in AI, semiconductors, digital infrastructure, advanced manufacturing and the energy transition.
On Thursday, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Malaysia's IMD ranking this year is proof that efforts to strengthen efficiency and improve execution continue to pay off. Malaysia continues to make progress in global competitiveness rankings, and the achievement shows the government's focus on improving efficiency and commitment is on the right track, he added.
The IMD ranking is based on four main pillars: economic performance, government efficiency, business efficiency and infrastructure. According to the IMD, the strengthening across all these pillars was the main factor driving Malaysia's improved standing this year.
Malaysia ranks fourth globally for economic performance, while government efficiency rose 11 places to 14th. Business efficiency also jumped 16 places to 16th, and infrastructure improved two places to 33rd.
Malaysia has steadily improved its competitiveness ranking over the past three years, climbing from 34th in 2024 to 23rd in 2025 and 15th this year.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Top Glove sees softer Q4 sales amid lower ASP, analysts say
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Top Glove Corp Bhd is likely to record weaker quarter-on-quarter (QoQ) sales volume in the fourth quarter of financial year 2026 (Q4 FY26), as customers remain cautious and adopt a wait-and-see approach.
This comes amid declining average selling prices (ASP) and expectations that prices could fall further before buyers begin replenishing their inventories, Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB) said.
"For pricing, in tandem with the subsequent decline in nitrile butadiene rubber (NBR) prices from its peak in early April, ASP for generic medical nitrile gloves appear to have peaked in May and have since trended lower for June and July orders," it said.
HLIB noted that NBR prices have continued to decline, while natural rubber prices have increased 6.3 per cent quarter-to-date.
"All in, we expect Top Glove to register weaker QoQ results in Q4 FY26, mainly dragged by lower sales volume and ASP.
"Profit margins are expected to contract meaningfully from third quarter (Q3) FY26 levels due to the lagged impact of higher-cost raw material inventories accumulated during the recent NBR price spike," the firm said.
CIMB Securities Sdn Bhd said Top Glove's ASP reached its peak in May 2026 before beginning to normalise in June 2026 as raw material costs eased.
"Based on our back-of-the-envelope calculations, we estimate ASP will decline by at least US$1 to US$2 per 1,000 pieces, but remain elevated compared with pre–US-Iran war levels (as current nitrile and butadiene prices are still 47 per cent and 12 per cent higher, respectively).
"We believe that a lower ASP environment may delay customer purchases, as buyers typically postpone orders in anticipation of further ASP declines," CIMB Securities said.
Following the Q3 results, the firm increased its FY26, FY27 and FY28 earnings forecasts by 79 per cent, 28 per cent and 24 per cent, respectively, after raising its assumptions for ASP as well as the US dollar-ringgit assumptions.
CIMB Securities said it has downgraded the stock to "Reduce" after the share price rally, which was driven by expectations of strong Q3 results, while maintaining its target price of 63 sen.
HLIB also increased its FY26 earnings forecast by 8.4 per cent, despite factoring in lower profit margin assumptions to account for the continued decline in NBR prices and ASP in Q4 FY26.
"However, we trim our FY27 to FY28 forecasts by 15.8 per cent and 8.4 per cent, primarily to incorporate our latest, lower in-house ringgit-US dollar exchange rate assumptions of 4.0 from 4.05 as well as higher natural gas prices in first quarter of financial year 2027," HLIB said.
The firm maintained its "Hold" recommendation while lowering its target price to 68 sen from 75 sen.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BTH: Johor's free-for-all contest & BN-PH face-off [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Johor state election is more than just a contest for 56 seats in the state assembly, it is shaping up to be one of Malaysia's most closely watched political tests, with implications that could extend far beyond the state.
In this week's episode, we look into the state's constituencies that are expected to be fiercely contested, making the election a barometer of voter sentiment ahead of the next general election.
Political analyst Professor Dr Azizuddin Sani joined the set.
He said a convincing victory would reinforce Umno's position as the dominant force in the unity government, while a weaker-than-expected performance could raise fresh questions about its grip on Malay support.
Although Barisan Nasional and Pakatan Harapan govern together at the federal level, he said how voters respond to this arrangement could offer valuable insights into public acceptance of Malaysia's evolving coalition politics.
From marginal seats and demographic shifts to party machinery and local issues, we discussed why Johor's election matters not just to Johoreans, but to all Malaysians.
Watch the latest episode on NST Online's YouTube channel.
* This episode was recorded on June 18
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Father, son injured in Klang house fire
Article:
KLANG: A father and son were injured after a fire destroyed 70 per cent of their double-storey terraced house in Taman Seri Istana Hills here early this morning.
Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ashrul Riezal Asbar said firefighters received an emergency call at 5.23am and dispatched personnel from the Klang Selatan Fire and Rescue Station to the scene.
"When the first fire engine arrived at 5.32am, it found the house on Jalan Raja Jumaat 17C/KS2 engulfed in flames.
"The fire involved a double-storey terraced house and caused damage to about 70 per cent of the structure.
"A 57-year-old man suffered burns to his wrists and head, covering about 10 per cent of his body, while his 28-year-old son experienced breathing difficulties due to smoke inhalation," he said.
Ashrul said both victims received initial treatment at the scene before being transported to Klang Hospital by the department's Emergency Medical Rescue Services (EMRS) unit.
He said firefighters brought the blaze under control at 5.47am using a 400-foot hose line with two water lines supplied from a nearby fire hydrant.
"The operation ended at 7.50am. A total of 13 personnel, assisted by two fire rescue tenders and an EMRS vehicle, were involved in the operation," he added.
The cause of the fire is under investigation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Maybank Islamic named Global Islamic Bank of the Year 2026 by The Banker
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Maybank Islamic Bhd has been named The Banker's Islamic Bank of the Year 2026, beating competition from financial institutions across the Middle East and Asia.
The recognition awarded by The Banker, an international financial affairs publication owned by the Financial Times, marks Maybank Islamic's return to the title after four years.
In its May/June 2026 edition, The Banker described Maybank Islamic as "the apex of global Islamic banking", balancing a RM390 billion asset base with cutting-edge ecosystem technology, positioning it strongly for future growth.
The award recognised Maybank Islamic's position as one of the world's established Islamic banks and the largest by assets outside the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) markets.
The publication also highlighted the bank's retail banking capabilities and focus on sustainability, with additional recognition as Islamic Bank of the Year for Sustainability and Retail Bank of the Year for Asia.
For the financial year ended Dec 31, 2025, Shariah-compliant assets made up 72.4 per cent of Maybank Malaysia's total loans and financing.
The bank also maintained about 30 per cent of Malaysia's Islamic banking asset market share.
The judges noted Maybank Islamic's expansion beyond conventional financing through integrated Shariah-compliant solutions and ecosystem-based offerings.
Among these initiatives was its halal facilitation programme, which saw more than RM369 million in Islamic financing disbursed in 2025 while supporting over 200 halal certification submissions.
Maybank Group Islamic Banking also mobilised RM34.54 billion in sustainable Islamic finance across Malaysia, Singapore and Indonesia in 2025, representing about 60 per cent of Maybank Group's overall sustainable finance.
Its social finance efforts were also recognised, with zakat distribution initiatives benefiting more than 89,000 households across Asean in 2025.
The Banker also highlighted Salaam Market, Malaysia's first Shariah-compliant business-to-business halal marketplace integrating trade, banking and ecosystem services.
Since its launch in late 2024, the platform has onboarded more than 9,000 buyers and sellers, generating RM8.3 million in transaction value and RM220 million in deposits.
The platform has since expanded to Singapore.
This latest award marks Maybank Islamic's fifth Global Islamic Bank of the Year recognition by The Banker since 2015.
Maybank Group chief executive officer of Islamic banking and CEO of Maybank Islamic Mohamad Yasin Abdullah said the recognition reflected the group's broader strategy and focus on Islamic finance.
"This achievement reflects the success of Maybank Group's M25+ strategy and our strong commitment and capability to leverage our regional leadership to deliver meaningful value to our customers, communities, and stakeholders," he said.
He added that Maybank Islamic would continue focusing on digital capabilities, sustainable finance and inclusive solutions.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Growing Rohingya presence raises concern in Perlis
Article:
ARAU: The rising number of Rohingya refugees in parts of Perlis has raised concern among residents, with a state assemblyman warning against complacency.
A survey found the community is scattered across at least four areas, including villages in Tambun Tulang and around Simpang Empat.
Most are believed to be working as labourers in the agricultural sector, with residents saying their numbers have increased.
A resident who identified himself only as Ali Abu, 65, said locals were worried by the growing presence of Rohingya in the state.
"The government should limit their entry. Some have married Malaysians and settled here. This needs to be addressed," he said.
He said concerns intensified after an estimated 300 Rohingya gathered during the recent Aidiladha celebrations.
The Rohingya are a predominantly Muslim minority from Myanmar's Rakhine State who have fled decades of persecution and violence.
The United Nations describes them as one of the world's most persecuted minorities.
Tambun Tulang state assemblyman Wan Zikri Afhtar Ishak said authorities should not be complacent, despite the situation remaining under control.
"There are Rohingya refugees in Perlis, but the situation is still manageable. The issue arises when they begin raising families here," he said.
He said he had raised the matter during a recent state assembly sitting and called for accurate data from the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) to facilitate monitoring of refugee arrivals.
Meanwhile, Rohingya refugee Mohamad Sadek Rashid Ahmad, 34, said he had lived in Malaysia since 2012 after arriving by sea via the Thai border.
The construction worker, who has lived in Perlis since 2013, said he holds a UNHCR card and married a Rohingya woman in 2022.
"I am grateful to the Malaysian people and government for allowing me to earn a living here.
"However, I am prepared to relocate if instructed to move to a third country," he said.
Data from the UNHCR website showed Myanmar nationals constituted the
highest number of refugees
in the country, with 193,824 Rohingya, 15,774 Chin, and 33,002 individuals from other ethnic groups.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia opens lower on profit-taking
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia opened lower on Friday as mild profit-taking in key index-linked counters weighed on early trade, despite overnight gains on Wall Street.
At 9.15am, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) eased 10.77 points to 1,700.62 from Thursday's close of 1,711.39, after opening 7.35 points lower at 1,704.04.
Market breadth was positive, with gainers outpacing losers by 215 to 193. A total of 296 counters were unchanged, 2,032 untraded and 34 suspended.
Turnover amounted to 274.46 million shares worth RM168.66 million.
Rakuten Trade Sdn Bhd vice-president of equity research Thong Pak Leng said Wall Street rebounded as sentiment steadied following a hawkish tone from the Federal Reserve on Wednesday.
He noted that the FBM KLCI ended flat at yesterday's closing and off the day's high of 1,722, attributed to late selling activities, presumably by foreign funds.
"Looking ahead, we believe trading will remain muted, thus anticipating the index to hover within the 1,705-1,715 range today," he said.
Among heavyweights, Maybank fell four sen to RM11.12, Public Bank slid 13 sen to RM4.87, Tenaga Nasional rose six sen to RM14.50, CIMB decreased eight sen to RM7.70 and IHH Healthcare slid three sen to RM8.74.
Among active stocks, Dagang Nexchange added 2.5 sen to 41 sen, Tanco was up half a sen to 15 sen, and Top Glove increased one sen to 73 sen. Sum Technology inched down 1.5 sen to 51 sen, while Borneo Oil was flat at half a sen.
Among top gainers, Malaysian Pacific Industries rose 94 sen to RM49.50, UWC gained 21 sen to RM6.32, United Plantations advanced 20 sen to RM32 and UMS was 18 sen firmer at RM8.88.
Top losers included Hong Leong Bank, which lost 22 sen to RM21.96, Paragon slipped 13 sen to RM1.58, Petronas Chemicals eased 12 sen to RM4.46 and Press Metal was nine sen weaker at RM8.43.
On the index board, the FBM Emas Index declined 55.29 points to 12,607.97, while the FBM Top 100 Index weakened 58.70 points to 12,444.91 and the FBM Emas Shariah Index dropped 14.39 points to 12,475.32.
The FBM Mid 70 Index rose 0.89 of a point to 18,091.70, while the FBM ACE Index gained 13.61 points to 4,773.88.
By sector, the Financial Services Index fell 197.14 points to 20,080.0, the Industrial Products and Services Index shed 1.0 point to 189.71, and the Energy Index erased 3.65 points to 758.77, while the Plantation Index rose 13.38 points to 8,780.83.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Retired lecturer dies after car crashes into wall
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: A 68-year-old retired public university lecturer died after losing control of his car, which skidded and crashed into a wall at Jalan Pahlawan yesterday.
The victim, Mak Kem Seng, was pronounced dead shortly after arriving at a private hospital here.
Kuala Muda police chief Assistant Commissioner Hanyan Ramlan said police received a call from the public regarding the single-vehicle accident at 4.05pm.
Initial investigations revealed that the victim was driving his Proton Wira from Jalan Lagenda Utama towards Jalan Badlishah.
"Upon reaching the scene, the car suddenly went out of control before skidding to the right side of the road and crashing into the entrance wall of the Kuala Muda welfare complex.
"The unconscious victim was rushed to a private hospital by ambulance but died while receiving treatment," he said in a statement today.
Hanyan said the case is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987.
The body was sent to the Sultan Abdul Halim Hospital forensic unit for a post-mortem, and the vehicle was taken to the Kuala Muda traffic police station for a Puspakom inspection.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Six rescued, three injured in early morning Melaka house fire
Article:
MELAKA: An elderly man and a woman suffered burns, while another man experienced breathing difficulties after they were trapped in a fire that destroyed a double-storey rented house in Taman Perkota Hang Tuah, Bukit Baru, early today.
All six occupants were rescued in the 3.30am incident at Jalan Permai 4.
The injured victims were taken to Melaka Hospital for treatment.
State Fire and Rescue Department operations division assistant director Mohd Shahrom Laji said the department received a distress call at 3.38am.
Fifteen personnel from the Ayer Keroh Fire and Rescue Station, assisted by the Bukit Katil Fire and Rescue Station, were deployed to the scene.
"When the firemen arrived, they found the double-storey house, which was shared by several tenants, already 80 per cent destroyed by the fire," he said in a statement today.
Shahrom said the burn victims were a 22-year-old woman and a 63-year-old man.
Another victim, a 26-year-old man, suffered breathing difficulties from smoke inhalation.
"The remaining three occupants, aged between 40 and 44, escaped unhurt," he added.
The fire was brought under control at 3.57am using auxiliary hose reels.
No fatalities were reported.
He said the cause of the fire and the total losses are still under investigation, and the state fire forensics unit is inspecting the site.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: #NSTviral: Myvi steals Lamborghini's thunder in viral toll plaza clip [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A Perodua Myvi has once again driven itself into Malaysia's unofficial 'king of the road' folklore after a viral video showed the compact hatchback emitting flames from its exhaust after a toll plaza.
The video, widely shared on Facebook, first showed a Lamborghini accelerating away from the toll plaza alongside other supercars, with 'afterfire' seen coming from their exhausts.
However, Malaysians online were more amused when the Myvi was later seen doing the same before zooming past several vehicles.
The location of the video could not be immediately verified.
The clip surfaced as the Kuala Lumpur International Mobility Show 2026 continues at the Malaysia International Trade and Exhibition Centre, where supercars, concept cars and mobility technology are among the attractions.
But online, it was the 'people's car' that stole the show.
Facebook user Clement Lee joked: "Remember folks, no matter how fast you are, there will always be a Myvi faster than you."
Another user, Zimie Zul, joked: "This is why I did not buy a Lambo. It would be embarrassing to be outdone by a Myvi."
The video has since become another addition to the long-running Malaysian joke about the Myvi's outsized reputation on local roads.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Shah Alam enters new chapter of growth
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Shah Alam prepares for the opening of the LRT3, the city is entering a new chapter of connectivity, accessibility and economic opportunity.
At the heart of the transformation is SA Sentral, a 58.68-hectare transit-oriented development strategically situated in Section 14, Shah Alam adjacent to the upcoming LRT3 Dato' Menteri Station.
Developed by Perbadanan Kemajuan egeri Selangor (PKNS), SA Sentral is envisioned as a catalyst for urban regeneration that will reshape how people live, work, connect and experience the city.
The development seeks to leverage the full potential of transit-led growth by integrating hospitality, commercial, residential, public spaces and community-focused destinations within a highly connected urban ecosystem.
As one of the largest urban rejuvenation initiatives in Shah Alam, SA Sentral project brings together key assets and future developments to create a vibrant city centre that supports economic growth, investment opportunities and sustainable urban living.
The project reflects a growing global shift towards transit-oriented developments, where mobility infrastructure serves as the foundation for more liveable, walkable and economically resilient cities.
"The upcoming LRT3 presents a once-in-a-generation opportunity to unlock the full potential of Shah Alam's city centre," said Datuk Mohd Najib Ibrahim, SA Sentral chief executive officer (designate).
"Through SA Sentral, PKNS is creating an integrated urban ecosystem that will strengthen connectivity, attract investments and support sustainable economic growth," Mohd Najib added.
Guided by the vision of Rejuvenate, Reconnect and Reinvent, SA Sentral is set to become a new gateway to Shah Alam and a key driver of the city's next phase of growth.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar, Turkmenistan president to hold talks to boost trade, investment ties
Article:
ASHGABAT (Turkmenistan): The second and final day of Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's official visit to Turkmenistan will be filled with a series of high-level engagements in the Central Asian nation, which lies along the eastern coast of the Caspian Sea.
He is scheduled to attend an official welcoming ceremony hosted by Turkmenistan President Serdar Berdimuhamedov at the Presidential Palace in the morning, followed by a one-on-one meeting.
Anwar and Berdimuhamedov will later have a bilateral meeting, followed by the signing of a joint statement and the exchange of agreements and memoranda of understanding (MoU) concluded by the two governments.
Both leaders are expected to discuss the current state of bilateral relations and explore avenues to further strengthen cooperation in trade and investment, oil and gas, agriculture and commodities, energy, tourism, education, digital, and science and technology.
They are also expected to exchange views on regional and international issues of mutual interest, particularly developments in Asean and Central Asia.
The prime minister and Berdimuhamedov are also expected to sign a joint statement and witness the exchange of several key documents, including an Air Services Agreement and a memorandum of understanding (MoU) between the foreign affairs institutes of both countries.
Both leaders will also witness the exchange of an MoU between the Transport Ministry and the State Service of Turkmenistan; an MoU between the Academy of Sciences Malaysia and the Academy of Sciences of Turkmenistan; and several documents in the field of oil and gas.
Anwar will then attend a luncheon hosted by Berdimuhamedov at the Presidential Palace before performing Friday prayers at the Turkmenbashi Ruhy Mosque.
Later, Anwar will jointly officiate the Malaysia-Turkmenistan Forum with Berdimuhamedov at the Chamber of Commerce and Industry of Turkmenistan, before holding a press conference with Malaysian media to conclude the visit.
Anwar is accompanied by Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani, Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir, as well as officials from the Prime Minister's Office and the Foreign Ministry.
Ashgabat, the capital of Turkmenistan, is globally renowned as the 'White Marble City' and holds the Guinness World Record for the highest concentration of white marble-clad buildings, boasting over 500 such structures.
The city is known for its monumental architecture, extensive gold-leaf detailing and broad, immaculate avenues.
Turkmenistan is bordered by Kazakhstan and Uzbekistan to the north, Afghanistan to the southeast, Iran to the south and southwest, and the Caspian Sea to the west.
The Caspian Sea is the world's largest inland body of water, and is technically an endorheic basin - meaning it has no outlets.
In 2025, Turkmenistan was Malaysia's fourth-largest trading partner among Central Asian nations.
Total trade between Malaysia and Turkmenistan amounted to RM75.80 million, with Malaysian exports valued at RM75.50 million, registering a 9 per cent increase compared with the previous year.
Turkmenistan is also a major investment destination for national oil company Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas), with the company's total investments in the country reaching RM52.73 billion since 1996.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: US sanctions Lebanese officials, Hezbollah-linked business network
Article:
WASHINGTON: The US sanctioned two Lebanese officials and companies Thursday allegedly linked to a Hizbollah business network, accusing them of obstructing Lebanon's peace process and delaying the disarmament of the militant group, Anadolu Ajansi (AA) reported, citing  statement from the US Treasury Department.
"Hizbollah must disarm for Lebanon to achieve a secure and prosperous future," Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said in a statement.
In a separate statement, the US State Department said "Hizballah is the single biggest obstacle to Lebanon's recovery and future, and holds the state hostage to a permanent state of conflict."
Earlier Thursday, US President Donald Trump said he expects a "complete ceasefire on all fronts," including Lebanon, Hizbollah and Israel, following the signing of a US-Iran agreement aimed at ending the Middle East conflict.
US Vice President JD Vance on Thursday criticised recent Israeli strikes on Beirut that disrupted negotiations with Iran, saying civilian casualties were "not acceptable."
Vance said that in the wake of the Iran deal's signing, the US expects Hizbollah to halt drone and rocket attacks on Israel and for Israel to refrain from "going wild in Lebanon."
Israel has been waging an offensive on Lebanon since March 2, killing 3,912 people, injuring 11,873 others and displacing more than 1 million, according to the latest official figures.
Israel continues to occupy areas in southern Lebanon, some for decades and others since the latest conflict between the two sides.
During the recent military campaign, Israeli forces advanced more than 10 kilometers (6.21 miles) into Lebanese territory.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Japan inflation unchanged in May after BOJ rate hike
Article:
TOKYO: Japan's core inflation was unchanged in May from April, government data showed Friday, days after the central bank hiked rates to a 31-year high because of the Middle East war.
Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's government has moved to shield consumers from the sharp rise in oil prices resulting from the three-month conflict with fuel and energy subsidies.
The year-on-year rise in "core" consumer prices last month in the world's fourth-largest economy of 1.4 per cent, which excludes fresh food, was in line with market expectations.
Excluding also energy, the reading was 1.8 per cent, down from 1.9 per cent and in line with market forecasts. Unadjusted, inflation inched up to 1.5 per cent from 1.4, also meeting forecasts.
The Bank of Japan (BoJ) raised its benchmark rate on Tuesday by 25 basis points to 1.0 per cent, the highest level since 1995 and marking the first increase since December.
The United States and Iran have agreed a peace deal to end their three-month conflict and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a vital waterway for oil and gas.
But the full resumption of traffic through the strait back to pre-conflict levels is expected to take considerable time.
The European Central Bank hiked rates last week after the war pushed up prices for oil and other goods worldwide.
The US Federal Reserve on Wednesday held rates steady.
But with US inflation at a three-year high, expectations are growing that its policy rate will rise later this year, despite pressure from President Donald Trump to cut.
Australia's central bank, which has raised borrowing costs three times this year, also held rates steady this week as did the Bank of England. Indonesia has hiked rates three times in four weeks.
Before the conflict began on February 28, Japan relied on the Middle East for around 90 per cent of its crude supplies.
Government subsidies has helped to keep core inflation under the BoJ's target of two per cent.
But the BoJ warned on Tuesday that there was a risk that inflation could accelerate past its objective as firms pass higher prices for raw materials onto consumers.
Marcel Thieliant at Capital Economics said that "there are no clear signs yet that higher energy costs are lifting prices of other goods and services".
"However, that is only a matter of time," he said in a note, forecasting inflation excluding fresh food and energy to rise to around 3.5 per cent in the first half of next year.
The yen has also come under major pressure in recent weeks, caused by the rise in oil prices and the gap between US and Japanese interest rates, pushing up resource-poor Japan's already colossal import bill.
Takaichi's government spent around 11.7 trillion yen ($72 billion) last month propping up the currency, which has been languishing at around 160 yen against the dollar.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: EU to develop new tools to tackle China export flood
Article:
BRUSSELS, (Belgium): EU leaders agreed during talks Thursday that the bloc must develop beefed-up trade defences to curb a surge of Chinese exports deemed an existential threat to European industry by Brussels — while simultaneously seeking "constructive dialogue" with Beijing.
There is a growing consensus in the European Union that it is too dependent on China, and Brussels fears this makes it vulnerable to potential coercion and supply shocks.
The 27-nation bloc's trade deficit in goods hit around 360 billion euros (RM1.7 trillion) last year, meaning Chinese exports sharply exceeded the EU's.
During a two-hour summit dinner in Brussels, leaders chewed over how the EU could address the imbalance, and whether the bloc needed to boost its arsenal.
After talks ended early on Friday, an EU official said the leaders tasked the commission with continuing to engage "in a constructive dialogue with our main economic partners" but did not directly say China.
They also asked the executive "to develop and eventually complement the toolbox in the area of trade defence" and to make sure the EU has "all the instruments it needs to defend its interests and derisk", the official said.
While EU capitals agree on a common diagnosis, the positions differ on the cure and several leaders on Thursday had called for dialogue as a priority.
Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez struck a more conciliatory tone than his counterparts before the dinner.
"We need friends, we need balanced relationships, we need to be pragmatic, and we need to build bridges both with major economies and potential allies, such as China," Sanchez told reporters.
- Following Trump's playbook? -
One way to beef up the EU's arsenal could be creating a new tool to impose sector-specific tariffs such as chemicals or green tech — taking a page out of President Donald Trump's playbook.
French President Emmanuel Macron last month called for a "European equivalent of Section 301" — the trade tool Trump has employed to set sweeping tariffs — arguing Europe's "sovereignty is at stake."
Germany has until now adopted a cautious posture because its economy is more exposed to potential retaliation, while Spain has sought to avoid tensions as it chases Chinese investment.
But Berlin appeared to be coming around to France's way of thinking.
A German official said Berlin was "open" to new tools if they are necessary so long as they were "not targeted at specific recipients."
German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, however, would not be drawn on China by reporters in Brussels.
Irish Prime Minister Micheal Martin said before the dinner he wanted to see "the shape and nature of any mechanisms" introduced but warned Europe had to make sure it understood the consequences.
The EU official did not comment on what any new tools would look like.
Concern about Chinese dominance is not limited to the EU.
Fears are rising in the West over Beijing's control in the market for rare earth minerals used in everyday electronic appliances, and China was on the agenda of G7 talks in France this week.
Brussels often evokes the need for fair competition, pointing to the unfair advantage Chinese companies have because of massive state subsidies.
Between 2005 and 2024, Chinese firms received around three to eight times more government support than firms in the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development, according to the OECD, which called it "a conservative estimate."
- EU appetite for a fight? -
Even as its resolve appears to be hardening, the EU has shown no appetite to trigger a broader trade war with China.
Fears over Chinese retaliation are not unfounded.
After the EU hit Chinese electric cars with higher tariffs in 2024, China imposed anti-dumping duties on European cognac.
And Beijing has vowed to hit back if the EU pushes through rules that would exclude certain products manufactured outside the bloc from public contracts.
Sefcovic has invited Chinese Commerce Minister Wang Wentao to Brussels later this month as the bloc still hopes it can prevent escalation through dialogue with China — but an EU official would not confirm the visit. — AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Stocks climb on chips, Brent gains on Iran deal doubts
Article:
NEW YORK: Major stock indexes climbed on Thursday as semiconductor shares jumped, while Brent oil touched its lowest level in weeks before finishing slightly higher.
An interim deal between the United States and Iran to end the Middle East war took effect, and oil tankers sailed through the Strait of Hormuz, pointing to a resumption of long restricted energy flows.
US Vice President JD Vance, however, warned Israel against further attacks on Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon, raising doubts about the durability of the US-Iran ceasefire agreement.
Brent crude futures gained 30 cents, or 0.38 per cent, to settle at US$79.85 a barrel after sliding as low as US$76.54 earlier in the session. US West Texas Intermediate fell 19 cents or 0.25 per cent to finish at US$76.60 a barrel.
Europe is more vulnerable to an increase in inflation from higher oil prices than the United States.
The US dollar index hit a one-year high after a hawkish tilt by the Federal Reserve on Wednesday raised bets on rate increases this year.
The Nasdaq gained 1.9 per cent and led gains on Wall Street, while the Philadelphia semiconductor index rose 6.4 per cent.
US President Donald Trump said iPhone maker Apple had agreed to work with Intel to design and manufacture its chips in the US. Intel shares jumped 10.6 per cent.
Peter Cardillo, chief market economist at Spartan Capital Securities in New York, noted the rebound in stocks from a day earlier.
"The reason for the optimism was Trump and the president of Iran signed the memorandum and oil prices fell," he said, adding: "There is enthusiasm that after 60 days a more solid deal will come to fruition and, by that time, hopefully the low supply factor in the oil market will be reversed."
DOLLAR FIRM
The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 72.15 points, or 0.14 per cent, to 51,564.70, the S&P 500 rose 80.48 points, or 1.08 per cent, to 7,500.58 and the Nasdaq Composite rose 496.28 points, or 1.91 per cent, to 26,517.93.
With the market closed on Friday for the Juneteenth holiday marking the emancipation of enslaved Black Americans, the S&P 500 showed a 0.93 per cent weekly gain compared with the Nasdaq's 2.43 per cent advance and the Dow's 0.71 per cent increase.
MSCI's gauge of stocks across the globe rose 6.48 points, or 0.58 per cent, to 1,127.60.
The pan-European STOXX 600 index fell 0.34 per cent.
In foreign exchange, the dollar index, which measures the greenback against a basket of currencies including the yen, euro and sterling, rose 0.45 per cent to 100.80, the highest level since May 2025. It surged 0.85 per cent the previous session, its biggest single-day jump in over three months.
The US central bank on Wednesday held rates steady in a 3.50 per cent to 3.75 per cent range as Kevin Warsh took charge with a sweeping policy review. The Fed funds futures market is pricing in 68 per cent odds of a rate hike by September, LSEG data shows.
The Japanese yen weakened as far as 161.45 per dollar, its lowest since July 2024, wiping out gains made after Tokyo's intervention on April 30. A break above the currency pair's 2024 high of 161.99 would send the yen to its weakest level since 1986.
Sterling fell 0.62 per cent to US$1.3206 after the Bank of England left interest rates unchanged.
US Treasury yields fell after rising the day before. The two-year yield, the most sensitive to Fed rate-change expectations, was down 1 basis point on Thursday at 4.153 per cent, after rising as high as 4.207 per cent on Wednesday. The 10-year yield was down 3 basis points at 4.437 per cent.
US gold futures fell 3.1 per cent to settle at US$4,245.9.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: TERAJU-backed Al-Meswak Mu'min emerges as Bumiputera halal FMCG success story
Article:
PENANG: Strategic support from TERAJU has facilitated the growth of Al-Meswak Mu'min into one of Malaysia's growing halal fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG) companies, culminating in a major manufacturing expansion.
The Bumiputera-owned company today marked the opening of its new manufacturing facility and launched its 25th anniversary celebration, underscoring a growth journey supported by RM15 million in strategic financing facilitated by TERAJU and Malaysian Technology Development Corporation (MTDC).
The new facility was officially inaugurated by Yang di-Pertua Negeri Tun Ramli Ngah Talib at Perda Food Park here.
The financing, channelled through the redeemable convertible preference shares instrument, enabled Al-Meswak Mu'min to invest in advanced manufacturing capabilities, expand production capacity and strengthen its competitiveness in Malaysia's rapidly growing halal consumer market.
As part of its expansion strategy, the company has also been identified to participate in TERAJU's Scale-Up Programme, aimed at strengthening its growth trajectory and enhancing its market competitiveness.
The expansion reflects TERAJU's broader strategy of identifying and scaling high-potential Bumiputera companies through targeted financing, business development interventions and capacity-building programmes aligned with the Bumiputera Economic Transformation Plan 2035 (PuTERA35).
TERAJU chief executive officer Junady Nawawi said Al-Meswak Mu'min represents the type of enterprise the agency seeks to nurture - a company with strong fundamentals, a differentiated market proposition and the potential to become a national industry leader.
"Strategic financing is not merely about capital injection. It is about enabling companies to build capacity, improve competitiveness and unlock new growth opportunities.
"Al-Meswak Mu'min demonstrates how the right support ecosystem can help Bumiputera companies scale their operations, strengthen their market presence and create long-term economic value," he said.
Junady said TERAJU had assisted eight other companies, all of which at different stages of progress.
"When TERAJU comes in, we do not only support companies that are already well-established. We look at companies that have existing revenue and some level of profitability, and our role is to help improve and elevate them further.
"We come in at the early growth stage, where the focus is not just on expansion but on bringing these companies to the next level. Our aim is to help these companies grow into stronger businesses that can attract larger investments from institutions such as Ekuiti Nasional Bhd (Ekuinas) and potentially other investors such as Permodalan Nasional Bhd (PNB) in the future.
"Ultimately, we want to bring these companies into the mainstream market and strengthen their position as competitive industry players," he added.
Founded in 2001, Al-Meswak Mu'min made its name as Malaysia's first halal-certified toothpaste manufacturer, entering a market then dominated by multinational brands and limited consumer awareness of halal personal care products.
What began as a single-product venture has since evolved into a diversified halal FMCG manufacturer with more than 100 stock-keeping units spanning oral care, personal care and household products.
Managing director Datuk Zaihal Hazri said the company's growth was built on a belief that halal principles should extend beyond food to products used in everyday life.
"Our journey began with a simple question - if consumers are concerned about halal food, why should the same consideration not apply to products used daily by families?
"That belief became the foundation of our business and continues to drive our innovation and expansion today," he said.
Zaihal said the strategic financing facilitated through TERAJU and MTDC was instrumental in helping the company realise its long-held ambition of establishing a comprehensive manufacturing hub capable of supporting future growth.
The new facility positions Mu'min as one of the few Bumiputera-owned manufacturers with integrated production capabilities across oral care, personal care and household products, enabling the company to increase capacity for both domestic and international markets.
"The new factory is more than a manufacturing facility. It symbolises 25 years of perseverance, innovation and belief that a home-grown Bumiputera brand can compete and thrive in a highly competitive market.
"From a single halal toothpaste product, we have grown to more than 100 SKUs today, and we remain committed to strengthening Malaysia's halal industry while expanding into new markets," he said.
Al-Meswak Mu'min is among the companies supported under TERAJU's Enhance and XCELERATE programmes, which focus on strengthening governance, operational capabilities and investment readiness among high-potential Bumiputera enterprises.
Its latest expansion supports broader efforts to deepen Bumiputera participation in the halal and FMCG sectors while advancing the aspirations of PuTERA35 to create more resilient, competitive and scalable Bumiputera companies.
As Malaysia seeks to reinforce its standing as a global halal hub, Al-Meswak Mu'min's growth story demonstrates how strategic financing, institutional support and entrepreneurial ambition can combine to produce a new generation of Bumiputera industry champions.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Leading through connection
Article:
SENIOR leaders are often judged by the quality of the results achieved by their organisations.
Yet, those results are rarely delivered directly by the leaders themselves. Instead, they are achieved through teams guided by middle-level managers who translate strategy into action.
For this reason, one of the most important responsibilities of any senior leader is not merely to issue directives, but to build strong relationships with middle managers, understand their realities and support them in delivering organisational goals.
Middle-level managers occupy a unique position within an organisation. They are close enough to frontline operations to understand daily challenges, yet senior enough to influence performance and culture. They act as the bridge between strategic intent and practical execution.
When senior leaders fail to appreciate the importance of this role, communication gaps emerge, priorities become confused and organisational effectiveness suffers.
Effective leaders recognise that directives alone are insufficient. While clear instructions are necessary, they achieve far greater impact when delivered within the context of trust and understanding.
Middle managers are more likely to embrace and champion organisational objectives when they feel respected, valued, and heard. This requires senior leaders to invest time in developing genuine relationships with them.
Being close to middle managers does not mean compromising authority or becoming overly familiar.
Rather, it involves maintaining regular contact, engaging in meaningful conversations, and showing interest in their experiences and perspectives.
Leaders who make themselves accessible gain a clearer understanding of the challenges faced by those responsible for implementing decisions. This insight allows directives to be more realistic, practical, and effective.
One of the greatest advantages of maintaining close relationships with middle managers is access to accurate information.
Organisational realities often differ from assumptions made at senior levels. Middle managers can provide valuable feedback on employee morale, operational difficulties, resource limitations and emerging risks.
Leaders who actively listen to this feedback are better equipped to make informed decisions and avoid unintended consequences.
Trust is a critical component of this relationship. Trust develops when middle managers believe that senior leaders genuinely care about their success and wellbeing.
It grows when leaders are consistent, transparent, and willing to listen. Once trust is established, communication becomes more open and honest.
Managers are more likely to raise concerns early, admit mistakes, and share ideas for improvement. This creates a healthier and more resilient organisation.
Understanding middle managers also allows leaders to tailor their communication more effectively.
Different individuals respond to different approaches. Some require detailed explanations and context, while others prefer concise instructions and autonomy.
Leaders who understand the strengths, motivations, and concerns of their managers can adapt their style accordingly. This flexibility increases engagement and improves the likelihood of successful execution.
Frequent interaction also enables leaders to identify and develop talent. Middle managers often represent the future leadership pipeline of an organisation.
Through regular engagement, senior leaders can observe potential, recognise strengths, and provide opportunities for growth. Coaching, mentoring and constructive feedback become more meaningful when founded on a genuine understanding of an individual's capabilities and aspirations.
At the same time, leaders must remain clear and decisive when issuing directives. Closeness should not create ambiguity regarding expectations or accountability. Effective leadership combines empathy with firmness.
Managers need clarity about organisational priorities, performance standards, and desired outcomes.
When expectations are communicated clearly, managers are better positioned to align their teams and make appropriate decisions.
The manner in which directives are delivered is equally important. Directives that are imposed without explanation can create resistance and frustration.
By contrast, leaders who provide context help managers understand the reasoning behind decisions.
Understanding the purpose of a directive often increases commitment to its implementation. People are generally more motivated when they understand not only what must be done, but also why it matters.
Successful leaders also recognise that communication should be a two-way process.
While directives may originate from the top, valuable insights frequently come from below. Encouraging discussion and feedback does not weaken authority; it strengthens decision-making.
Middle managers who feel comfortable sharing their views can help identify potential obstacles before they become significant problems.
Another important benefit of staying close to middle managers is the ability to monitor organisational culture.
Middle managers have a profound influence on employee attitudes and behaviours. They shape workplace experiences through their daily interactions with staff.
Leaders who maintain strong connections with these managers are better able to assess whether organisational values are being upheld and whether desired behaviours are being reinforced throughout the organisation.
Periods of change particularly highlight the importance of these relationships. During uncertainty, employees often look to their immediate managers for guidance and reassurance.
Middle managers, in turn, look to senior leaders for clarity and support. Leaders who have already established trust and understanding are better positioned to guide their organisations through challenging periods.
Their directives are more likely to be accepted because they are delivered within an environment of credibility and confidence.
Leaders who remain distant from middle managers often encounter avoidable difficulties.
Decisions may be poorly implemented, concerns may remain hidden, and morale may decline. Distance can create misunderstandings and foster an environment where managers feel disconnected from organisational leadership.
Over time, this weakens both performance and engagement.
By contrast, leaders who actively cultivate relationships with middle managers create a stronger foundation for organisational success.
They gain better information, foster trust, improve communication, and enhance execution. Their directives are more likely to be understood, accepted, and implemented effectively because they are grounded in mutual respect and shared understanding.
Ultimately, effective leadership is not simply about giving instructions. It is about creating the conditions in which others can succeed.
Senior leaders who take the time to know their middle managers, understand their challenges, and support their development are far more likely to achieve lasting results.
By combining clear direction with genuine connection, they transform middle managers from mere implementers of strategy into committed partners in organisational success.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MAG sees stronger transit traffic through Kuala Lumpur
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Aviation Group (MAG) is seeing strong passenger demand on Europe-bound services via Kuala Lumpur driven in part by travellers from Australia and New Zealand connecting through the Malaysian capital.
MAG president and group chief executive officer Captain Nasaruddin A. Bakar said Malaysia Airlines Bhd's load factor on the Kuala Lumpur-London route had exceeded 95 per cent.
"In the first few months (March, April) because of the closure of Middle East airports, we're seeing a lot of flow especially from Australia, New Zealand to Europe via Kuala Lumpur.
"I think it's important for Malaysia Airlines and also for Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to ensure that we are ready for that kind of movement," he told Business Times in an exclusive interview recently.
Malaysia Airlines has also operated additional flights to London to meet demand, although slot constraints at Heathrow Airport currently limit the introduction of additional scheduled services.
"We have some extra flights to London. Having due to slots reason, we're not able to induce additional schedule flight into London at this point of time," Nasaruddin said.
He added that Malaysia Airlines, the Transport Ministry, Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB) and the Civil Aviation Authority of Malaysia (CAAM) have been working closely to support the country's aviation industry and strengthen Kuala Lumpur's competitiveness as a transit hub.
"As a company, we have been working a lot with the government through the Transport Ministry, CAAM and MAHB," he said.
Nasaruddin also said Malaysia Airlines must ensure seamless network connectivity, including providing efficient transit connections between Australia and Europe through Kuala Lumpur with transit times of around three hours.
"As an airline, we have to ensure that we have additional product to entice people to stop over Kuala Lumpur rather than to Singapore or Bangkok," he added.
Citing an example, Nasaruddin said Malaysia Airlines is offering the Bonus Side Trip programme, which offers complimentary flights to selected domestic destinations for eligible international passengers travelling through Malaysia.
He said airport infrastructure also plays an important role in attracting transit passengers and ensuring a seamless travel experience.
"As an airport, we have to ensure that our infrastructure is seamless. It means the infrastructures are well and good, have been maintained, are operationally well. That will help the international customers flying to Kuala Lumpur rather than Bangkok or Singapore," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MAG to decide on A350 replacement plans by year-end
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Aviation Group (MAG) expects to make a decision by year-end on its future widebody fleet, including replacement plans for Malaysia Airlines Bhd's Airbus A350 aircraft.
MAG president and group chief executive officer Captain Nasaruddin A. Bakar said the assessment is ongoing as the group evaluates its long-term fleet requirements and hopes to conclude the review in the fourth quarter of this year.
"It's still ongoing. We're doing a lot of assessment for the fleet replacement for the A350s…Analysis is still ongoing. We're doing the assessment and we have not made any decision on that," he told Business Times in an exclusive interview recently.
Meanwhile, MAG's new aircraft delivery programme remains on track with the group expecting to receive 11 aircraft this year comprising of both Boeing B737 Max 8 and Airbus A330neo (new engine option).
Nasaruddin said MAG has already taken delivery of six aircraft so far this year, comprising two A330neos and four B737 Max aircraft.
He added that so far, the group has not received any indication from either Airbus or Boeing that future aircraft deliveries will be delayed.
"From our side, there's no delay. From the manufacturers, so far there's no indication that there's any delay from Airbus and Boeing," he said.
In Aug 2022, MAG signed an agreement with Airbus for the purchase of 20 A330neo aircraft with options for an additional 20 jets as part of its long-haul fleet renewal programme.
In July 2025, the group exercised its purchase rights for the additional 20 A330neo aircraft. Deliveries for the additional batch are scheduled between 2029 and 2031.
While MAG continues to assess its future passenger widebody fleet requirements, Nasaruddin said the group currently has no plans to purchase new freighter aircraft to replace MASkargo, the cargo arm of Malaysia Airlines, fleet.
"The passenger demand especially in Asia Pacific is quite steady. For the cargo demand, we're seeing stability of the cargo and we know that moving forward cargo will be something that we have to focus on."
"Currently, there is no plan to do any replacement of the A330 cargo aircraft," he said, adding that MASkargo continues to strengthen partnerships through collaborations with Qatar Airways Cargo and IAG Cargo to expand its cargo business.
When asked if MAG would consider the A350F, the next-generation widebody freighter aircraft, Nasaruddin said it is not part of the plan.
"No, currently we're not looking into that. That's not part of the plan," he said.
As for Flyfirefly Sdn Bhd (Firefly), Nasaruddin said the airline's fleet plan remain unchanged and there is no plan at the moment to replace its turboprop aircraft.
The airline currently has a fleet of five B737-800 aircraft and nine ATR 72-500 turboprop aircraft.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: May trade signals more upside for Malaysia
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's trade may have remained on a strong footing in May, reinforcing expectations that the country's record-breaking momentum could extend into the coming months despite global uncertainties.
The Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry said early indications pointed to another positive month ahead of the official release of May trade data by the Statistics Department today (June 19).
"The May trade figures will be released soon, and based on the indications we have seen so far, the performance remains very positive," its deputy minister Sim Tze Tzin told reporters after the Malaysian International Chamber of Commerce and Industry's 53rd annual general meeting today.
The country's total trade surpassed the RM1 trillion mark within the first four months of 2026, reaching the milestone at the fastest pace on record.
Total trade rose 15.3 per cent year-on-year (YoY) to RM1.127 trillion, driven by a 19 per cent increase in exports to RM609.31 billion, while imports expanded 11.1 per cent to RM517.4 billion.
The trade expanded 28.6 per cent YoY in April, the fastest growth since September 2022, to RM336.73 billion, driven by strong export demand across key markets and products.
Exports rose 36.9 per cent to a record RM182.74 billion, marking the 10th consecutive month of expansion and surpassing the previous high of RM152.77 billion recorded in December 2023 by RM30 billion.
Imports increased 20 per cent to RM153.99 billion, resulting in a trade surplus of RM28.75 billion, extending Malaysia's streak of trade surplus to 72 consecutive months since May 2020.
The strong export performance was mainly supported by double-digit growth in major export products, led by electrical and electronic (E&E) products, which climbed RM28 billion to a new record high amid robust demand from the artificial intelligence (AI) and automotive electronics segments.
Sim said the strong showing underscored the resilience of Malaysia's external trade sector despite mounting geopolitical tensions and disruptions to global supply chains linked to developments around the Strait of Hormuz.
"Malaysia has recorded its highest-ever trade figures, with April marking the strongest trade performance in the country's history.
"This shows that Malaysia is resilient. We have also achieved double-digit growth with many of our major trading partners around the world. Among them are the United States, China, Taiwan, Peru, South Korea and Japan.
"We have recorded double-digit growth with these key markets, and we believe this positive trajectory will continue for the next few months," he said.
Sim attributed part of the strong performance to the global AI supercycle, which continues to drive demand for Malaysia's E&E exports.
While optimistic about the near-term outlook, he said sustaining the momentum throughout 2026 would depend on developments in the global economy.
"Whether this momentum can be sustained through 2026 remains to be seen, but I am cautiously optimistic given the rapidly changing global environment," he said.
Economists told Business Times that Malaysia's trade outlook from June will depend on several key factors including global demand conditions, commodity price movements and the stability of international supply chains.
Universiti Teknologi Mara Business Management Faculty senior lecturer Dr Mohamad Idham Md Razak said key factors influencing the outlook include the pace of recovery among major trading partners such as China and the US.
He added that global electronics demand trends and movements in commodity prices, including crude oil and palm oil, would also play an important role.
"Additionally, geopolitical developments and trade policies could either support or disrupt export flows.
"On the domestic front, exchange rate movements and Malaysia's ability to sustain competitiveness in key sectors such as E&E, manufacturing and commodities will also play an important role," he said.
Idham said export growth is expected to maintain a steady pace, with certain sectors showing resilience, rather than experiencing a significant acceleration.
He added that easing geopolitical risks and improvements in global supply chains could provide support, but uncertainties surrounding global economic growth and demand may continue to weigh on the outlook.
IPPFA Sdn Bhd director of investment strategy and country economist Mohd Sedek Jantan said the recent moderation in geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, coupled with the decline in crude oil prices, could provide support for global trade conditions.
He said lower energy costs would help reduce transportation and production expenses, ease inflationary pressures, and potentially encourage stronger consumer spending and business investment worldwide.
"This should benefit Malaysia's export-oriented sectors, particularly manufacturing," he said.
Sedek said risks remain, including continued uncertainty over US trade policies, possible tariff actions and weaker growth prospects in major economies, which could contribute to bouts of volatility.
"Nevertheless, barring a significant deterioration in the global economic environment, the overall outlook remains constructive, and I expect Malaysia's export growth to maintain a stronger trajectory in the coming months," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: UN demands RSF halt 'imminent offensive' in Sudan
Article:
GENEVA: The United Nations on Thursday called on RSF paramilitaries to halt their "imminent offensive" on the strategic Sudanese city of El-Obeid, warning of catastrophic consequences for civilians.
"Stop this madness," said UN rights chief Volker Turk, following reports of a significant troop build-up by the Rapid Support Forces and allied troops around El-Obeid, as well as intensified drone strikes and artillery shelling.
El-Obeid, the capital of North Kordofan state, sits along a key route linking RSF-held areas in the western Darfur region to army-controlled regions in the east.
Sudan's conflict erupted in April 2023 between the regular army and the RSF.
For months, El-Obeid has been partially encircled by paramilitary forces, in what Turk's office described as siege-like conditions.
Turk said an imminent offensive risked serious crimes being committed, as well as deepening the catastrophic impact on the civilian population.
"We have seen this playbook before," Turk said in a statement.
He said the world cannot "allow a repeat of the preventable atrocities" documented during RSF offensives on the city of El-Fasher and the Zamzam displaced persons camp in North Darfur state last year.
'STARK WARNING TO THE WORLD'
"Civilians are at grave risk in Kordofan, particularly in El-Obeid, in the absence of action to halt the imminent offensive and further military escalation," said Turk.
"The states with influence have the duty to exercise it now to stop this madness in its tracks."
The conflict in Sudan has killed tens of thousands of people and forced more than 11 million from their homes, creating what the UN describes as the world's largest displacement and hunger crises.
Fighting has intensified in recent months in the Kordofan region and Blue Nile state near the Ethiopian border, particularly after the RSF captured El-Fasher in October, the army's last major stronghold in western Darfur.
The UN's independent fact-finding mission on Sudan in February concluded that the siege and capture of El-Fasher had inflicted "three days of absolute horror" and bore "the hallmarks of genocide."
SUDANESE 'NEED PEACE'
Kordofan – home to oil deposits, arable land and the RSF's most powerful paramilitary allies – remains a key and fiercely contested battleground.
Turk said the "ever-increasing use of drones" to conduct airstrikes was having a "devastating impact" on civilians in Kordofan.
At the UN Human Rights Council in Geneva, 29 countries said they were "gravely alarmed by the urgent risks of atrocities and deliberate killings in Sudan", and called on the RSF to "immediately cease their assault on El-Obeid."
"We are deeply concerned at the risk of imminent escalation on the ground, leaving approximately 500,000 civilians at risk of falling victim to large-scale atrocities, including more than 100,000 internally displaced persons," they said, in a joint statement.
The 29 countries included Australia, Britain, Canada, France, Germany, Norway and Spain.
"The people of Sudan need peace,"
"Urgent measures must be taken to protect civilians and prevent further atrocities," said Turk.
"The world is watching, and those responsible for violations must be held accountable." - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Paris awards honorary citizenship to Palestinian civilians, journalists
Article:
PARIS, France: The City of Paris on Thursday granted honorary citizenship to Palestinian civilians and journalists, in a symbolic gesture of support for "the suffering the Palestinian people."
"Honorary citizenship is not just a symbol, but a commitment to peace. We are extending a hand to an entire people," Emmanuel Gregoire, the capital's Socialist mayor, said before the Council of Paris.
He spoke in the presence of the Palestinian representative in France, Hala Abou-Hassira, who received a long round of applause.
"Recognising the suffering of the Palestinian people in no way erases that of the Israeli people," said Gregoire.
"We will never forget Oct 7, 2023," he added, stressing that the French capital had earlier granted honorary citizenship to the hostages of Palestinian group Hamas.
A resolution adopted by the Council of Paris said "the humanitarian situation of Gaza's population remains dramatic."
The right voted against the resolution, pointing to a resurgence of antisemitic acts in France.
Hamas's Oct 7, 2023 attack on Israel resulted in the deaths of 1,221 people on the Israeli side, most of them civilians, according to an AFP tally based on official data.
It sparked the war in Gaza, where a ceasefire in effect since October last year has largely halted fighting.
The war has reduced much of the Palestinian territory to rubble, with an estimated death toll of more than 73,000 people, the majority of them civilians, according to the health ministry in the Hamas-run territory, whose figures are considered reliable by the United Nations.
Last week, Paris hosted a meeting of Palestinian and Israeli civil society groups, who urged global leaders to take urgent action and help implement a permanent ceasefire.
France as well as Britain, Canada and several other countries recognised a Palestinian state last year. Paris has earlier bestowed honorary citizenship on the people of Nagorno-Karabakh and the Ukrainian capital Kyiv.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cabinet approves National Tahfiz Council, Zahid to chair new body
Article:
KUANTAN, June 19 — The federal government has approved the establishment of the National Tahfiz Council, marking a major step in streamlining the direction, education model, recognition standards and academic pathways for
tahfiz
students nationwide.
Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the approval was given at last week’s Cabinet meeting.
He said approval for the establishment of the council was given at last week’s Cabinet meeting.
“The Prime Minister (Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim) asked me to be the chairman (of the National Tahfiz Council). Insya Allah, with everyone’s support, it will be a major step forward,” he said when addressing the Pahang State Huffaz Gathering 2026 at Yayasan Pahang yesterday.
The inaugural event successfully brought together more than 5,000 huffaz from across the state.
Meanwhile, the Deputy Prime Minister expressed hope that the establishment of the National Tahfiz Council would position
tahfiz
education as a recognised educational pathway within the education system.
“I want
tahfiz
students to be able to progress from madrasah to university, from memorisation to skills, and from tahfiz institutions to the professional world with confidence, qualifications and a wider future,” he said.
Ahmad Zahid said Pahang had demonstrated that
tahfiz
education could be developed into a complete knowledge pathway, beginning in early childhood, progressing through schooling and extending to the international level.
“For example, efforts to instil a love for the Quran from an early age have been implemented in Pahang, inspired by the Sultan of Pahang (Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri’ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah), through Tadika Tahfiz Negeri Pahang.
“From this initial foundation, Pahang is building a more structured and progressive
tahfiz
education pathway that holds greater meaning for the future of huffaz children,” he said.
Meanwhile, Ahmad Zahid, who is also the Rural and Regional Development Minister, said the government is reviewing the implementation of improvements to the National Tahfiz Policy 2.0 starting this year.
“Within this framework, several key initiatives have been introduced, including TVET Tahfiz, the Malaysian Tahfiz Certificate 2.0, the Graded Hafazan Certification, the Huffaz Financing Scheme, the Malaysian Tahfiz Recognition Standard, as well as strategic collaborations with higher learning institutions and skills institutes,” he said.
At the event, Ahmad Zahid also witnessed the exchange of a memorandum of understanding between Yayasan Pahang, the Community Development Department (Kemas) and Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) as a step to strengthen
tahfiz
education development and expand skills development opportunities for huffaz. — Bernama

Title: Tengku Zafrul eyes Selangor seat in GE16, points to Pandan vacancy and Ampang groundwork
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — Senior Political Adviser to the Prime Minister, Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Tengku Abdul Aziz, has proposed that he be given the opportunity to contest a seat in Selangor in the 16th General Election (GE16).
He said the proposal had been submitted to the PKR leadership for consideration and approval by the party’s president, Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
“I have put forward a proposal that, should there be an election, particularly the general election, I be given the opportunity to contest in Selangor, but no final decision has been made,” he said.
Tengku Zafrul said this when met by reporters after attending the Yayasan TZA Appreciation Ceremony here yesterday.
The PKR Ampang division member said he did not personally have any preferred constituency in mind and would leave it to the party leadership to decide whether to field him in any parliamentary seat that becomes available.
“As a party member and a member of the Ampang division, I spend a great deal of time in Ampang, Pandan and several other areas where I have been asked to assist.
“In Pandan, there is a vacancy as there is currently no Member of Parliament, and I have also been asked to monitor developments in Ampang as the party’s representative,” he said.
On May 30 last year, Tengku Zafrul, who previously contested the Kuala Selangor parliamentary seat under the Barisan Nasional (BN) ticket in the 15th General Election (GE15), announced his resignation from Umno and subsequently joined PKR.
PKR vice-president Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari later announced Tengku Zafrul’s appointment as the party’s monitoring officer for the Pandan and Ampang parliamentary constituencies, in line with the party’s move to designate Pandan as a special constituency under the supervision of Anwar.
Previously, former Economy Minister Rafizi Ramli announced his departure from PKR and vacated the Pandan parliamentary seat. — Bernama

Title: Treat us unfairly and developing nations will deal elsewhere, Anwar tells Europe
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has warned that developing countries may seek alternative partners if European nations continue to treat them unfairly, citing a defence-related dispute involving Norway.
He said Malaysia had paid 95 per cent of the contract value of the defence-related procurement but was given no satisfactory explanation for Norway’s decision despite repeated efforts to seek clarification.
The episode would influence Malaysia’s future decisions, Anwar said.
“If this is how European nations treat us, and the others are completely muted and silent, then we will have to seek alternatives, not to deal with them,” he said in an exclusive interview with, RT, a Russian television network.
The Norwegian government reportedly revoked the export licence for the Naval Strike Missiles (NSM) system last March, just days before the scheduled delivery, following tighter export controls on sensitive military technologies.
Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said on June 2 that the unilateral decision had caused Malaysia direct losses exceeding RM600 million for the missile procurement, while indirect costs could surpass RM1 billion.
Anwar said Malaysia had initially suspected the issue could have been linked to Nato and assistance from the United States.
“We thought initially, we did suspect that probably because of Nato and the assistance of the United States of America.
“But Nato countries, Germany, France in particular, and Italy, said no, we are still having that relation with Malaysia,” he said.
He stressed that Malaysia had honoured its contractual obligations by paying 95 per cent of the agreed amount.
“But here there was an agreement signed. We paid 95 per cent, paid, but this is total nonsense.
“To me, it’s unacceptable, it is pathetic to have a country like Norway, having excellent relations in the past, to take a decision, and there was no explanation,” he said.
